title: midterm exam update
content:
Hi Everyone,

We plan to release the midterm grades Sunday evening. There are several things to keep in mind, so please read this entire message carefully.

  1. For every question on the exam, the correct answer was the one selected by most students. I will go over the top three questions that proved to be the most challenging in the Monday lecture. Afterwards, please follow up with your TAs about any other questions you've been wondering about.
  2. While the class overall did very well, there is a very broad range of individual grades -- ranging from many people scoring As to unfortunately a few Fs. If you did well, congrats! If you wish you did better, keep in mind that there are still notable opportunities to improve your grade, such as the outstanding Research Report assignments and the Final Exam. Most importantly, double down on your efforts to attend the class regularly, take good notes, and engage with the lectures and discussions. People who have been doing so generally did better on the exam.
  3. Rather than haggling over any specific exam question and to help everyone succeed in the class, (1) we will add the equivalent of 3 exam points (i.e. a question worth bump to the exam grade) to the class grade under a separate grade rubric (i.e. Midterm Exam Extra Points) in the coming days. (2) There will be an extra credit opportunity assignment in the coming weeks. Stay tuned for the assignment details.

Best,

Dr. Tir and TA Team
contentText:
Hi Everyone,
We plan to release the midterm grades Sunday evening. There are several things to keep in mind, so please read this entire message carefully.

For every question on the exam, the correct answer was the one selected by most students. I will go over the top three questions that proved to be the most challenging in the Monday lecture. Afterwards, please follow up with your TAs about any other questions you've been wondering about.
While the class overall did very well, there is a very broad range of individual grades -- ranging from many people scoring As to unfortunately a few Fs. If you did well, congrats! If you wish you did better, keep in mind that there are still notable opportunities to improve your grade, such as the outstanding Research Report assignments and the Final Exam. Most importantly, double down on your efforts to attend the class regularly, take good notes, and engage with the lectures and discussions. People who have been doing so generally did better on the exam.
Rather than haggling over any specific exam question and to help everyone succeed in the class, (1) we will add the equivalent of 3 exam points (i.e. a question worth bump to the exam grade) to the class grade under a separate grade rubric (i.e. Midterm Exam Extra Points) in the coming days. (2) There will be an extra credit opportunity assignment in the coming weeks. Stay tuned for the assignment details.

Best,
Dr. Tir and TA Team
author: Jaroslav Tir
